title: alcea rhyticarpa var. tiliacea
intro: Alcea rhyticarpa var. tiliacea, also known as the Hollyhock, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family Malvaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, where it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating skin irritations, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as sore throats, wounds, and respiratory problems.
